entry.title: Re: SAP Enable now metadata could not be found.
entry.content:
@issam7 wrote:

could you tell where to find metadata of SEN in BTP after cloud team uploaded it based on requirement in a raised ticket?

Hello @issam7,
To find the metadata of SEN in SAP BTP after the cloud team uploads it, navigate to your BTP subaccount. Go to Security > Trust Configuration, and download the SAML metadata file. If unclear, consult the cloud team or SAP documentation for specific guidance. Big Lots customer survey

Best Regards,
David Ball

entry.title: A Little Feedback Can Go a Long Way
entry.content:
When was the last time you provided feedback? I feel that giving feedback is something that we are asked to do on a constant basis. Your work colleague needs feedback on their project, your friendly neighborhood coffee shop wants you to give them a review. Most restaurants, cafes or hotels literally live off good reviews. It is essential to their business and their reputation and personally, I don’t think I can remember the last time I went to a restaurant without first checking the reviews it has online. Requests for feedback seem to be omnipresent, especially in the digital world. And most of the time I feel overwhelmed or annoyed and decide not to provide it at all.

However, since attending our most recent expert breakfast by SAP Women in Tech I have reconsidered my annoyance a little bit. The topic was of course feedback and how it can lead to product improvement. The breakfast session emphasized that not all feedback is constructive and some of it can be quite curious. But there is also feedback from users that is very helpful to the product teams. Let’s say enough users complain about a missing feature they can set the ball rolling. This can be something as simple as receiving a notification about some request being declined. And trust me it sounds small, but nothing is more cumbersome than having to access a tool several times just to see if your request has been accepted or denied. You want that information in your inbox or maybe even on a dashboard to see.

I’ve learned from bespoke breakfast, that even the small things can make a huge difference in simplifying peoples lives. I know all those pop-ups and requests to provide feedback can be terribly annoying when navigating the digital world. But next time you encounter something that could use a simple improvement, why don’t you provide that feedback.

entry.title: Re: Purchased & Assigned SAP Learning Hub Subscription to...
entry.content:
Hi Margit,

Last update I got on my support case (5000131653) based on my questions was:

The A_ACT_2404 assessment is included SAP Learning Hub subscription customer edition but in “SAP Certification exam, two attempts” subscription.  I'm sorry we're not the right team who can handle SAP LH purchase issues.

I have contacted the SAP LH team again via another support request on Digital Commerce Platform. In case I need to purchase another, new License/Subscription do you happen to know where I can purchase the referenced License: SAP Learning Hub subscription customer edition?

On SAP Learning Hub I see only three type of exam subscriptions: (I purchased the first one)

  1. SAP Certification exam, two attempts: Two attempts plus 10 practice hours
  2. SAP Certification exam, one attempt: One exam attempt to get certified
  3. SAP Certification exam, six attempts: Six exam attempts to get certified

Thanks for your support.   Regards Armin

entry.title: The termination occurred in ABAP program "/TMWFLOW/SAPLCROSS_SYSTEM_LOCK", in "SYSTEM-EXIT"
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

Please help me here.

when developer trying to change the transport layer in development packages and the TADIR entries for the objects to make them “home” in the SAP system TI1.

But when try to get in to change mode, they get an error with the RCF call to the PUM SolMan System

The SU53 shows nothing regarding this problem.

 

But when look ,where to problem comes up, it is at a RFC call to the SolMan for checking of locked objects.

I tried to check RFC in TI1 system and from my end it looks OK. Both connection and authorization test are OK.

From the screen below it looks like more authorization issue as is visible on the screen from su53.

 unfortunately, I couldn’t find any cross-system object locks in PUM system and don’t have user account in TI1 system to check the transport requests.

But observed the ABAP dumps for these locks, the termination occurred in ABAP program "/TMWFLOW/SAPLCROSS_SYSTEM_LOCK", in "SYSTEM-EXIT". The main program was "SAPMSSY1".

I have attached the ABAP dump file from Tcode ST22. Please help here.

Regards

Srinivas V

entry.title: Re: EWM: Planned replenishment with layout-orientated war...
entry.content:
Hi @Hansdadad ,

"But I want this to be taken into account, has anyone here had any experience?" - Yes I had experienced this both normal RF picking and with ASRS. I trust pick point is activated at ST-Y500, I am sure LOSC would trigger while confirm task in RF and move to pick point. Again you would control things below Whole parameter (Partial Removal ) then Pick point, process define would be like that. Full pallet Picking - Skip LOSC...

"A picture for simplification:" -> Pick Point is the solution...example return once picking done for partial quantity.

"However, if I select the line and want to create the warehouse task, none is created because the system correctly recognises that the MAIN-HU (pallet) may not be stored in the small parts warehouse (container warehouse)." -> I saw that you kept the HU Type Validation, try to skip for a moment...check the process please. I would suggest create WT then check via RF and let us know if any questions. 

Also create Pick point Work center....assign bin...

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/52c8cb53ad377114e10000000a174cb4.html

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Navigating SSL Certificate Download and SAP Regulatory Content Service Configuration
entry.content:
In today's digital landscape, ensuring secure communication and compliance with regulatory standards is paramount for businesses. This blog post aims to guide you through the process of downloading SSL certificates and configuring SAP Regulatory Content Service for seamless integration and compliance management. 

Downloading SSL Certificates 

SSL certificates are crucial for establishing secure connections between web servers and browsers. To download an SSL certificate using popular browsers like Chrome or Edge, follow these steps: 

  1. **Access the Website**: Open the website for which you need the SSL certificate. 
  2. **Locate the Lock Icon**: In the address bar, click on the "lock" icon. This icon indicates a secure connection. 
  3. **View Certificate Details**: Click on "Certificate" or "View Certificate" to access detailed information. 
  4. **Download the Certificate**: Save the certificate locally by selecting the appropriate option. 

For more detailed instructions, refer to SAP Note 2853519 (https://me.sap.com/notes/2853519) - How to download the SSL certificate of SAP BTP. 

 

Executing Background Job SAP_EHFND_PC_CONTENT_LOAD 

The background job SAP_EHFND_PC_CONTENT_LOAD, as mentioned in SAP Note 3344198 (https://me.sap.com/notes/3344198) - S4TWL - Enablement for continuous delivery of listed substance content, can be executed without a direct connection to the SAP Regulatory Content Service. This job has a fallback mechanism that loads missing data from the MIME repository if no connection is maintained in the customizing activity "Specify Communication Settings for Integration into External Systems Functions." 

However, establishing a connection to the SAP Regulatory Content Service is highly recommended. This ensures regular content updates without the need to update your S/4HANA release. For configuration details, refer to SAP Note 3055767 (https://me.sap.com/notes/3055767) - Configuration of the SAP Regulatory Content Service for Product Compliance and Environment, Health, and Safety Management. 

 

API Consumption and Registration 

APIs are included to connect your SAP system to integrate data from outside sources to enrich your compliance processes. To begin, you must register on the SAP Developer Hub. Use the following URL for the registration process:

https://contentservice-live.apibhubenterprise.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com

entry.title: Re: SAP Utilities Roadmap 2025 Onwards
entry.content:
Hello Amit,

In case that your customer requirement is only about creating the technical master data and will not become much more in the future, the by Michael mentioned OData API is the very good candidate to implement this. Via the IS-U master data generator you can easily default specific ISU fields (e.g. rate category) and it is also possible to create various templates in case that you have to create different master data. Depending on the complexity to determine the values of the IS-U objects you can do this completely without any customer development.

Let me know which solution you will finally implement.

Regards,

Pieter

entry.title: Re: SAP BODS/IS upgrade from 4.2 SP14 to 4.3 SP03
entry.content:
Hi Joe,

Thank you for your response.

We are currently using BIP in our landscape for the CMS functionality, and I hope it is ok to continue using it with the 4.3 SP4 Patch 8 version as per the note 3197694).  As IPS is just a trimmed version of the BIP, and customized for the data services.

Upon checking the SAP Note: 3197694 with the comparability matrix, I see below are active and compatible versions( Please check the attachment.)

DS/IS 4.3 SP03 Patch 1 + BIP 4.3 SP4 Patch 3-8

I hope my understanding is correct.

 

Based on these, we have downloaded the following Files for the BIP, DS, and IS

For the remaining steps, like upgrade, migration and resync, we would be following the similar steps that you have mentioned.

Can you please comment on the planned target versions and downloaded files?.

 

Thank you in advance,

entry.title: SAP Best Practices for Advanced Shipping & Receiving
entry.content:
With SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, we have delivered two SAP Best Practices scenarios for Advanced Shipping & Receiving.

7GP – Basic TM/EWM – Inbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving.

7GQ - Basic TM/EWM – Outbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving.

Advanced Shipping and Receiving (Adv SR) is introduced to harmonize the processes between the different logistic modules in SAP S/4HANA:  

  • SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain for transportation management (in this document further referred to as “TM”) 
  • SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain for extended warehouse management (in this document further referred to as “EWM”) 
  • Stock Room Management in SAP S/4HANA (STRM) 
  • Inventory Management and Physical Inventory in SAP S/4HANA (MM-IM)  
  • Logistics Execution in SAP S/4HANA (LE) 

Advanced Shipping and Receiving is an integrated, end-to-end process that allows users to send, receive, and transport products. The new simplified integration supports mixed and multi-warehouse scenarios within one single freight order (FO). It helps users coordinate the process steps from arrival of trucks at the gate to the final goods receipt in the warehouse. Now, a truck that has posted arrival at checkpoint can carry out both loading and unloading activities at a location, or it can deliver goods from several warehouses at the same location. Information moves freely between the components and allows you to gain insights into the status of the products during the process. 

Communication is simplified by using a harmonized data model, by applying the EDIFACT standard to EDI messages, and by providing dedicated apps and expanding existing apps and RF transactions for shipping and receiving processes. Information moves freely between the components and allows users to gain insights into the status of the products during the process.

The key benefits of the new ASR process is to harmonize processes in Inbound and Outbound for better visibility and more efficient execution. This involves:

  • Reduce redundancy in business objects.
  • Consistent user experience for execution processes independently of warehouse applications.
  • Harmonized Data.
  • Closer Integration between Transportation and Warehouse activities.
  • Better Alignment with Industry Standards.

Scope Item 7GP describes a common inbound scenario for an Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) ordering material from a supplier at different times during a certain time span. In this case study the Adv. SR scenario runs in the OEM’s facilities with a basic TM license and an Extended Warehouse Management license.

The below images describe the process flow for Inbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving scenario covered with Scope Item 7GP.

Inbound Process - 1

Inbound Process - 2

Scope Item 7GQ describes a common outbound scenario for an Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) receiving multiple orders from customers. In this case study, the Adv. SR scenario runs in the OEM’s facilities with a basic TM license and an Extended Warehouse Management license.

In the outbound process of Advanced Shipping and Receiving we distinguish between two major process variants i.e. Transportation Driven Process & Warehouse driven Process. Both these processes have been included as part of the 7GQ scenario.

Transportation Driven Process

 A transport-driven process prioritizes transportation planning while warehouse activities are blocked until manually released.

A transport-driven outbound process takes place in the context of internal logistics integration between Transportation Management (TM) and Delivery Management as well as TM and Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), Lean Warehouse Management, and Stock Room Management in SAP S/4HANA. A transport-driven execution prioritizes transportation planning. After delivery creation, the delivery is blocked for warehouse execution until transportation preparations have begun and a block on warehouse activities has been removed. When you release the delivery for warehouse processing, it is then unblocked, and warehouse execution can start.

You must set the warehouse processing status to Ready for Warehouse Processing manually. Consequently, the warehouse receives the information needed to plan and execute its warehouse activities accordingly.


Warehouse Driven Process

In this kind of scenario, warehouse activities (in LE as well as S/4 HANA EWM) are blocked until the planning process in S/4 HANA TM is finalized and the dispatcher has set the status “ready for warehouse processing” in S/4 HANA TM manually. This means the warehousing tasks will be executed only after transportation planning in TM.

The below screenshots describe the process flow for Outbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving scenario covered with Scope Item 7GQ.

Outbound Process - 1Outbound Process - 2

The below screenshot highlights the document hierarchy when performing an inbound scenario in Transportation Management.

Document Flow - Inbound Scenario

The scenario starts with the creation of Purchase Order. Once the supplier has the goods packed and ready for shipment, an Advanced Shipping Notification (ASN) is sent to the OEM’s system via DELVRY IDOC. The ASN message will trigger the creation of an Inbound Delivery. The creation of the inbound delivery in SAP S/4HANA also triggers the automatic inbound delivery creation in an embedded SAP EWM system. At the same time the system also generates freight unit for the inbound delivery.

The logistics user can use these freight units to already get a rough overview of incoming orders for a dedicated location including e. g.  the relevant Warehouse Number and Warehouse Door or the current Warehouse Processing Status. 

The Transportation Planner uses the freight units to generate a Freight Order.  Once the Freight Order is created, the Transportation execution takes place. In this process trucks arrive at the receiving point location, a warehouse door is assigned to them and then unloading of goods is done. After unloading the trucks are checked out and departed from the receiving point location.

Going forward, we plan to release the Advanced features of Transportation Management with Advanced Shipping and Receiving.

For more information on SAP Best Practices for #SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, please visit
Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

entry.title: Early billing in Phased Contract in BRIM
entry.content:
Required to bill 2 phases of a phased contract together in CI. 

Eg.

Phase 1: 01.01.2025 to 30.06.2025 

Phase 2: 01.07.2025 to 31.12.2025

Here the scenario is Billing in Advance. The Start of Billing date is 01.01.2025, for both the phases, but the 'Valid from' and 'Term from' is '01.07.2025', 'Next Request Date' in phase 2 is 01.07.2025 so the BITs cannot be requested until 01.07.2025

Although 'Start of billing date' is given, it is not reflected on BIT creation. Not sure if anything else is missing. Any solution to this scenario? Thanks in advance.

#BRIM #SOM #CI

entry.title: EWM for MRO and Other materials
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a process in which warehouse operation is responsible for receiving both non-stock items like consumables and also raw materials. My requirement is to use one fiori app for both processes. 

For non-stock items GR will be done based on the PO and for the others we will be doing a GR with an inbound delivery i.e. for raw materials. 

Any standard app I can use to perform GR against a PO and an inbound delivery?

entry.title: Unable to Retrieve Business Data in Custom Inbound API on BO – S/4HANA Cloud 2502 (RAP)
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to create custom inbound API based on TUTORIAL from developers.sap.com. The problem is that i can't get data from my system on Partner Demo Customizing tenant (100) for S4H Cloud Public Edition 2502 to test it. When i try use option Data Preview i got below message. 

Additionally, when i released service binding based on CDS Projection View for I_SupplierInvoiceTP to validate it, the symptoms were the same - table was empty. ...

My activities: 

  • In my opinion i have enough business roles assigned because i have an access to Manage Sales Order application where i can search for created documents. Maybe not enough? - if so, where i can find information about necessary business roles that i need to assign to my account on S4Hana system ?

  • CDS has released contracts C1 and C0.  

  • What's more, in my Partner Demo Development tenant (080) i have an access to preview data. But it's a development system without business data so i can't test my Business Object.

Does anyone know solution that will help me to get an access to business data to test my custom inbound API based on BO ? Frankly, i am run out of concepts what i can do more... 

Best regards, AN

entry.title: Re: Custom logic for storage location determination?
entry.content:
Hi @JaiSrinivasan 
We have a requirement from business related to ABC for Plant substitution, where we are not able to meet using Standard Building rules to determine best alternative. Hence planning to go for BAdI suggested by you BADI_ATP_ABC_ALTV_DETMNR to implement custom logic. Could you please suggest how can we build logic inside this determination to meet below customer requirement.

1) System should look for one alternate plant only when the ATP quantity (Available stock) in original plant is less than 30%. Alternate plant with full confirmation has to be substituted with Inline substitution.
2) All the materials have RLT active which was challenging for Standard Building rules to work as it is considering Confirmed quantity as 100% with future date.

Really appreciate your support or guidance on this requirement.

Regards
Carsten

entry.title: Re: BTP ADS Migration Neo Environment - Cloud Foundry
entry.content: Good Content. Informative. !!

entry.title: Re: android.permission.READ_MEDIA_IMAGES and android.perm...
entry.content:
Hallo Alek, In SAP Mobile Services, specifically for the SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) Android apps, several permissions are requested by default. These permissions are essential for various functionalities within the app. Below are some of the key permissions and their use cases:

Required Permissions

  1. android.permission.CAMERA

    • Use Cases:
      • Scan QR codes during onboarding
      • Scan barcodes using barcode scan action, search bars, and simple property barcode scan input
      • Take a picture and attach within the application
    • Recommendation: It is advised not to disable this permission as it is crucial for the mentioned functionalities 
       

  2. android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION

    • Use Cases:
      • SDK Map and Usage features
    • Note: This permission is not currently exposed in out-of-the-box MDK controls/features. If your app does not try to get the current location nor use the SDK Map or Usage, it could potentially be disabled 
       

  3. android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE

    • Use Cases:
      • Selecting files or images for attaching within the app
      • Reading offline OData stores
    • Note: You can disable this permission if your app does not offer file attachment and offline data access features 
       

Disabling Permissions

To disable a default permission, you can create a partial manifest in your .mdkproject as shown below:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android" xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools">
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION" tools:node="remove" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" tools:node="remove" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" tools:node="remove" />
</manifest>

Note: Disabling a necessary app permission can break your app's functionality 

 

Security Recommendations

If your app manages files such as downloaded attachments or images captured using the device camera, consider how these files are stored and how they can be passed to external viewers. Android provides content providers that you can use to manage this data. For more information, see Content providers 

Additional Resources

For more details on image assets and configuring permissions for your app built with SAP Build Apps, refer to the Image Assets and iOS Usage Strings guide 

If you have any further questions or need additional assistance, please feel free to ask.

entry.title: Re: Hana Migration Cockpit Direct Transfer Error
entry.content: Hello, I had the same error as the screen shot even though the profit center field was filled. After further analysis I found that the instance in the valuation data sheet was mapped to an additional plant that was not maintained in the plant data sheet for the same instance (This issue was not shown during the validation). After adding the additional plant in the plant data sheet the issue was solved. You can check your instance for other maintained views as well.

entry.title: Re: SAP Gardener: Cultivating a Thriving Digital Ecosystem 🌱🚀

This would run on a weekly schedule.

Explaining errors and recommending fixes on data loads, something else?

entry.title: Re: SAP Buld Process Automation. Error: "Subscription Fai...

Hello Peter, Please click the "View Details" button next to the error. This will provide more information about any missing entitlements, which should help clarify the issue.

Let me know if you need any further assistance!

entry.title: Re: Use currentUser.email as filter in Basic table with d...
entry.content:
To use the current user's email as a filter in a basic table with a data adapter in SAP, you can follow these steps:

Steps

  1. Retrieve Current User's Email:

    • Use the sap.ushell.Container.getService("UserInfo").getEmail() method to get the current user's email address. This method returns the email of the logged-in user. javascript var userEmail = sap.ushell.Container.getService("UserInfo").getEmail();

  2. Apply Filter in Data Adapter:

    • Use the retrieved email to apply a filter in your data adapter. This can be done by setting the filter parameters in your table binding. javascript <Table id="idExternalFindingTable" updateFinished="onupdateFinished" inset="false" growing="true" growingThreshold="5" items="{ path: 'maindata>/Findings', parameters: { expand: 'assignedTeamUsers' }, filters: { path: 'user_userEmailId', operator: 'EQ', value1: userEmail }}"> </Table>

Example Code

Here is an example of how you can implement this in your SAP UI5 application:

// Retrieve the current user's email
var userEmail = sap.ushell.Container.getService("UserInfo").getEmail();

// Apply the filter in the table binding
<Table
  id="idExternalFindingTable"
  updateFinished="onupdateFinished"
  inset="false"
  growing="true"
  growingThreshold="5"
  items="{ path: 'maindata>/Findings',
           parameters: { expand: 'assignedTeamUsers' },
           filters: { path: 'user_userEmailId',
                      operator: 'EQ',
                      value1: userEmail }}">
</Table>

entry.title: Message mapping: disabled fields still show in target message
entry.content:
Hi,

In integration suite i create a message mapping (graphical). My targetmessage is an ORDERS05 idoc.
I noticed that the fields ARCKEY, DOCNUM, CREDAT en CRETIM (all in EDI_DEC40) all get values even if i don't have any mapping to then.
I then disabled the fields but they are still visible when i execute the mapping, and they get a value. 
This is weird behaviour in my opinion. 

Can anyone explain? Or offer a solution?

Kr
Robert

Thanks for your help with this!

entry.title: Re: Balance in transaction currency is too large for auto...
entry.content:
Hello Jeremy,

I checked the post and saw that there is an SAP note (3080961 - SAP Fiori: Tolerance check with maximum percentage rate) that makes an adjustment.

Since our environment is Public Edition, the note is already applied.

I'm debugging this tolerance code to see if I can identify what's wrong, but without success so far.

entry.title: Re: C4C Service - Service & Repair scenario: How are requested start and end date determined?
entry.content: It is independent of SLA.But it Request start date=Creation date+2 days, Request End Date=Request Startdate + 1 Day

entry.title: Re: How can I change the name of BP
entry.content:
• Has the business partner been created from a tool such as Workforce Person Replication?
  • Do you really want different information between employee and business partner?
  • Have you tried the 'Manage Business Partner' application, the 'Maintain Business Partner' application or the 'Manage Workforce' application?

Depending on your answer try something such as

  1. Log in to SAP Fiori launchpad in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system as a Configuration Expert - Business Process Configuration (SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT).
  2. Open the Manage Your Solution app.
  3. On the Configure Your Solution screen, filter the list using these values:
  • Application Area: Human Resources
  • Subapplication Area: Organization
  • Item Name: Value Mappings

entry.title: Nested IF Statement Comparing Versions
entry.content:
Hi all,

I want to implement the following logic: Write a "1" to Measure = Mismatch_Indicator for every member in "Site" Dimenstion if sales are above zero in Version = Public.Z AND there are no sales in Version = Public.X. 

The idea is to flag this site.

The only difference on these two versions, is that in version Public.Z, Sales are placed on d/DIM1 = "#" and d/DIM2 = "#". Both are placed on a Site.

I have tried with the following script, however I don't get the desired output.

CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = ON

//SCOPE
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "Mismatch_Indicator"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = [d/Version].[p/From_Date] TO [d/Version].[p/To_Date]

//DATA WRITING

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Net_Sales_ext", [d/Version] = "public.Z", [d/DIM1] = "#", [d/DIM2] = "#") > 0 THEN
IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Net_Sales_ext", [d/Version] = "public.X") = 0 THEN
DATA([d/Measures] = "Mismatch_Indicator") = 1
ENDIF
ENDIF

entry.title: Re: Joule for Developers: Word Search Challenge
entry.content:
UNITTESTGENERATION

 

This will save a lot of time for developers, as Joule automatically is going to be able to generate ABAP unit tests for ABAP classes and CDS code.

entry.title: Re: How to reach out to SAP Education - Learning Hub team for support
entry.content: @SAP_Krish thank you for letting me know... I am glad it has been resolved. Please contact us via the CIC channels mentioned above in case you need something else. Have a great day!

entry.title: Re: My Inbox Showing Blank
entry.content: Yeah I did check that Tcode and reconfigured the that and I seem not to get any information where the following are still Blank:

entry.title: Re: Xsteps in Process order. How to add custom logic to s...
entry.content:
Hi Andrew

While I don't want to disregard the possibility of using a BAdI implementation (perhaps with a clever filter value), I would like to know why you actually need two alternative SXS references? If the purpose of this is to let the user select the applicable resource, the user could instead choose a different production version which then determines a different resource. Would that not be possible? 

Jan

entry.title: Re: Balance in transaction currency is too large for automatic correction
entry.content:
I'm also looking at this post (https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/a-simplified-guide-to-effectively-use-the-clearing-api/ba-p/13721805) to solve my issue.

In that example, the author passes two document types, RE and KZ (payment and invoice entries), in the payload.

In my scenario, I'm only passing the RE document.

Do I need both document types to fully clear an open item?

entry.title: Hi I need to avoid copying of characteristics from one DIR version to another version
entry.content:
Hi
in SAP DMS
when i create new version for Document (DIR), Characteristics & the values are copied from previous version to next version.

I need to avoid copying of characteristics from one DIR version to another version when its created.
PLM Document Management System (DMS) PLM Classification

entry.title: Re: How to create a DB connection to HANA Data Lake in SL...
entry.content: The error indicates that there is a problem with your certificate.

entry.title: Country dimension for Expense Reporting not working for Subledger and Concur
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We have business requirement of reporting operating expenses by Country.

Recently, SAP has enabled new additional accounting dimensions in general ledger line items and analytical reports needed for insurance carriers: product group, branch, data source, customer country/region, customer group, customer industry.

However, there are certain limitation with the Country/Region field as listed below:

- Field country is not available for Fiori app 'Create Supplier Invoice'

- Value in country field is updated in Concur and same is getting populated in Concur payload file. However, it's not getting filled in S4 accounting document. We tried to explore Substitution rule for Concur via Fiori app 'Manage Concur Substitution Rules' to update the same in S4 accounting document, but field Country is not available in target field of substitution rule.

- Similarly, we are facing issue to update country value for Fixed Assets posting. We are unable to update it for depreciation posting. We tried to build substitution rule for Fixed Asset Posting via Fiori app 'Manage Substitution/Validation Rules for Asset Posting', but field country is not available in target field of substitution rule. 

Any inputs would be appreciated.

Regards,

Virendra Sharma

entry.title: Re: Job Information Manager Self-Service Now Displays Filtered Events Based on Event Reason Permissi
entry.content: I assume this change will only impact those who do not use Event Derivation business rules?

entry.title: Microsoft .NET dependencies on SAP Business Objects 4.3
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Hope you are doing well!

We see the SAP BO 4.3 server uses the below Microsoft .NET applications, and the Microsoft .NET Framework is see as one of the pre-requisites while performing the BO Install/Upgrade.

  • Microsoft .NET 6.0.36 - Windows Server Hosting
  • Microsoft .NET Runtime - 6.0.36 (x64)
  • Microsoft .NET Runtime - 6.0.36 (x86)
  • Microsoft ASP .NET Core 6.0.36 - Shared Framework (x64)
  • Microsoft ASP .NET Core 6.0.36 - Shared Framework (x86)

My current BO version is BI 4.3 SP04 Patch 900, but I see that the .NET 6.0.36 already reached it's EOL on Nov 2024, but still used in the server. May I know what is the exact dependency of these applications on SAP BO. If they have minor dependencies, can they be removed or upgraded?

Please suggest?

entry.title: Boosting HR Productivity with Employee and Manager Self-Service
entry.content:
In today’s evolving workplace, HR teams face increasing demands to streamline processes, improve data accuracy, and enhance employee experiences. One of the most effective ways to achieve this is through Employee and Manager Self-Service (ESS & MSS) functionalities in SAP SuccessFactors (SF). By enabling employees and managers to perform routine HR tasks independently, organizations can reduce administrative burdens, ensure compliance, and improve HR efficiency.

Key Considerations for Implementing Self-Service in Your Organization 

 

 

 

1. Technology Considerations

  • System Access & Availability: Ensure all employees, including deskless workers, have digital access to SAP SF via desktop kiosks, mobile apps, or Microsoft Teams integration.
  • Integration with Other HR Systems: Ensure Employee Central integrates seamlessly with payroll, performance, and learning modules to maintain data consistency.
  • User Experience (UX) Optimization: Configure Quick Actions, role-based permissions, and SAP Work Zone widgets for an intuitive and frictionless user experience.

2. Process Considerations

  • Identify Key HR Transactions: Prioritize high-impact processes like employee data updates, time-off requests, and manager-initiated promotions for self-service automation.
  • Optimize Workflows: Review approval chains, business rules, and validation checks to ensure streamlined yet compliant self-service transactions.
  • Ensure Data Accuracy: Implement data validation rules and audit logs to maintain integrity and compliance with HR regulations.

3. Policy Considerations 

  • Self-Service Governance: Define who can access, edit, and approve HR transactions to align with internal policies.
  • Security & Compliance: Ensure compliance with GDPR, ISO 27001, and SOC 2 security standards for employee data privacy.
  • Change Management Strategy: Proactively address resistance by engaging employees, providing training, and communicating the benefits of self-service adoption.

4. Resource Considerations 

  • HR & IT Collaboration: Ensure HR and IT teams work together to configure Quick Actions, test integrations, and support self-service adoption.
  • Training & Support: Use SAP WalkMe for in-app guidance, FAQ sections, and training materials to assist employees and managers in navigating self-service functionalities.
  • Continuous Improvement: Regularly assess self-service adoption, collect user feedback, and implement improvements based on analytics insights.

How SAP SuccessFactors Empowers ESS & MSS

SAP SuccessFactors provides a centralized, cloud-based platform where employees and managers can access and update HR data anytime, anywhere. The key functionalities include:

Employees can manage their personal records efficiently, eliminating dependency on HR teams for routine tasks:

Managers gain visibility into their team’s data and can execute HR-related actions directly through the system:

ESS & MSS Accessibility Across Platforms

SAP SuccessFactors ensures a seamless multi-platform experience, making self-service accessible:

  •  Web-Based Portal: Employees and managers can access SuccessFactors via a standard browser with a user-friendly UI.
  • Mobile App: The SAP SF Mobile App (iOS & Android) allows users to complete transactions anytime, anywhere.
  •  Microsoft Teams Integration: Quick Actions and HR requests can be initiated directly within Teams.
  • SAP Work Zone: Provides a digital workspace with HR dashboards, alerts, and self-service actions.

Optimizing ESS & MSS with Quick Action Cards

One of the most powerful innovations within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central is Quick Action Cards — a streamlined, user-friendly way to execute HR transactions instantly.

What Are Quick Action Cards?

Quick Actions are pre-configured, simplified workflows that enable employees and managers to initiate and complete HR tasks with fewer clicks. They appear on the SAP SF homepage, reducing navigation complexity and increasing efficiency.

Use Cases for Quick Actions:

  •  Employee Quick Actions:
    • Update Address – Employees can change their address instantly without navigating complex forms.
    • Edit Banking Information – Modify payroll details securely.
    • Submit Time-Off Requests – Request leave in just a few clicks.
  • Manager Quick Actions:
    • Promote an Employee – Quickly initiate a promotion request, select new job details, and submit for approval.
    • Create a New Position – Duplicate existing roles or create a new job position with minimal input.
    • Change Employee Job Info – Modify direct reports' job titles, pay grades, or work locations.

Integration of Quick Actions with AI-Powered Chatbots

SAP’s Joule AI companion integrates with Quick Actions, allowing users to execute HR transactions via chat. For example, a manager can simply ask Joule to "promote Jada", and the system will prompt relevant fields, apply default settings, and initiate approval workflows.

The Business Case: Realized Benefits & ROI

Organizations that adopt SAP SuccessFactors ESS & MSS with Quick Actions report:

  • 50-75% reduction in HR administrative workload – by shifting transactional tasks to employees and managers
  • Time savings of 3-5 minutes per transaction – enabling HR teams to focus on strategic initiatives
  • Significant cost savings – one case study showed a mid-sized enterprise saved over $1.5M annually by optimizing manager workflows

Best Practices for Implementing ESS & MSS with Quick Actions

  • Assess Your Current Maturity Level – Determine how much self-service is already in use and identify gaps where automation can drive efficiency.
  • Make Transactions Easy to Find – Use homepage Quick Actions, SAP Work Zone, or mobile-friendly configurations to ensure employees and managers can quickly access key tasks.
  • Provide User-Friendly Navigation – Leverage role-based permissions to present only relevant tasks to different user groups, simplifying the interface.
  • Empower with Mobile Accessibility – Ensure all ESS & MSS features are available on the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile App to cater to deskless workers. Leverage AI for Smarter Workflows – Integrate Joule AI companion and Quick Actions for seamless task execution.
  • Monitor and Optimize – Use People Analytics to track usage, completion rates, and process efficiency to continuously improve the self-service experience.

Employee and Manager Self-Service is a game-changer for HR efficiency. With SAP SuccessFactors and Quick Action Cards, organizations can drive productivity, accuracy, and employee engagement, transforming HR from a reactive function into a strategic powerhouse.

Is your HR team ready to embrace the future of self-service? Now is the time to leverage SAP SuccessFactors to unlock efficiency and business value.

The original webinar & slides are available on-demand here: https://play.goconsensus.com/ued51434f?fn=Egle&ln=Pociute&em=egle.pociute@sap.com&co=SAPSuccessFactors

 

Additional Resources

Simplifying Employee and Manager Experiences with Employee Central Quick Actions

Boost Efficiency and Productivity by Activating AI Functionalities in Employee Central

Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Quick Actions

Using stories in People Analytics Templates

Use Cases for Employee Central Quick Actions Differences Between Quick Actions and Self-Service Transactions | SAP Help Portal

We are committed to support all of our SAP SuccessFactors customers on their individual Path to Success. These sessions are part of a wide range of ways we can support your evolution to continuous value and driving business outcomes.  

 

#pathtosuccess #employeecentral #quickaction

entry.title: Re: Generation of MPIDs for the SAP ABAP system

would you mind explaining what an MPID is at all?

Thanks, Boris

entry.title: Re: Cost Center and Profit Center Hierarchy Not Appearing...
entry.content:
Did you already look for some KBA such as

  • 3538690 - F1581: Hierarchy not available for Cost centers in Plan Cost Centers on Periods app (F1581) 
  • 3521574 - Profit/Cost Centers Hierarchy filter missing for reports 
  • 3559612 - Cost center hierarchy missing in reporting Apps 

Basically execute 'Set report relevancy' app or transaction code HRY_REPRELEV, checkboxes 'Report Relevant' and 'Auto-Replicate'

entry.title: Re: Report ZCLEANUP_PASSWORD_HASH_VALUESX - Remove weak password hash values
entry.content:
Hi Frank,

thank you for this blog and the report.

If I use your report to remove week password hashes from table USH02 why should this irritate auditors as we will document this cleanup and only the password hash is removed from the table so that the rest of the change document still exists ?

Kind regards,

Arnd

entry.title: Re: Hi Priyanka,Just like UPDATE_INSERT, Do SAP CPI suppo...
entry.content:
Hi Sakshi..

To my knowledge, currently there is no direct equivalent operation like DELETE_INSERT(delete or insert at the same time). However you can achieve similar functionality by implementing a sequence of below steps in your integration flow by making sure the logic and exception handling are maintained properly. 

1. checking/validation of the existing object

2. delete/remove if necessary and

3. create/post/insert 

Hope it helps 🙂

entry.title: Re: Support package stack updates for SAP ABAP systems: Software Update Manager
entry.content:
Hi Mani,

I may have overlooked something, but I do not understand why you copied an existing stack file instead of creating a new one. That would rather be "from scratch" - my view.

Thanks, Boris

entry.title: Re: Translation icon is not shown for some of published c...
entry.content: 'The United States and Great Britain are two countries separated by a common language' (George Bernard Shaw)

entry.title: Re: Adobe Forms: table incl. header and title is not correctly shown
entry.content: Hi, did you manage to find a solution for it? i'm facing exactly the same issue

entry.title: Re: Hana Migration Cockpit Direct Transfer Error
entry.content: Hello, I had the same error as the screen shot even though the profit center field was filled. After further analysis I found that the instance in the valuation data sheet was mapped to an additional plant that was not maintained in the plant data sheet for the same instance (This issue was not shown during the validation). After adding the additional plant in the plant data sheet the issue was solved. You can check your instance for other maintained views as well.

entry.title: Re: SAP BODS/IS upgrade from 4.2 SP14 to 4.3 SP03
entry.content: @MekhaMahendraprabhath, BIP should also work just fine and yes the versions you highlighted are compatible and should work.

entry.title: Re: Xsteps in Process order. How to add custom logic to s...
entry.content:
Hi Jan,

much appreciate your feedback as i'm a bit stuck with just one direction and your comment gives an option for another angle to look at...

The original idea of the flow is that user is balancing the order in PP/DS and moving between 2 alternative resources which are created in respective operation classification. So once one or antoher resource is selected, needed SXS is generated. 

If we try to review your direction:  Process order is changed which triggers some ehnancement point which triggers re-read PP master data and selecting needed PV based on the resource in subject operation. That could be an option as well i suppose.

However as this task is part of a bigger integational project between SAP Control Recipe based on Xsteps and some external system, i'm trying to figure out which method could be used to trigger some custom logic which could control Control Recipe and Xsteps content...  in particular changing nodes and steps of predefined Xsteps.. 

Thanks a lot!

Kr, Andrew.

entry.title: Re: 1H 2024 Product Release - How to copy a role with the...
entry.content: It is available, just in case you still need it.

DigiCert Global Root G3

DigiCertTLSECCP384RootG5

entry.title: Re: Editable CL_SALV_TABLE after release 756
entry.content:
Hi All , 

Can someone confirm if we can make the selected rows editable with this , as i have implemented this and all fields are getting editable for all rows expectation is it should only make the fields of selected rows editable.

entry.title: Not Classified Error while Printing Labels in EHS Global Label Management
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

While printing labels for a new material in EHS GLM, the contents of the label are not being printed, rather the text "Not Classified" is shown on the print. The necessary label data is already maintained at the material master level including generation variants, label stock, etc.

Some areas of the print are fine, the screenshot is also attached.

Could someone please guide how to rectify this issue?

entry.title: manifest file has two different application configured but only one tile appears in Build Workzone
entry.content:
Hi All,

Background: I have one sap ui5 application which launches two different application based on custom parameter.

Requirement : I want this application to be deployed in SAP Build Workzone as two separate applications.
There are two crossNavigation added to the manifest.json file of the project. 

Issue: However only one project shows up in SAP Build Workzone. Can someone suggest how to fix this.

"crossNavigation": {
"inbounds": {
"atom-ui-claims-inbound": {
"signature": {
"parameters": {
"appmode":{
"defaultValue": {
"value" : "claims"
}
}
},
"additionalParameters": "allowed"
},
"semanticObject": "Claims",
"action": "manage",
"title": "Claim Management",
"subTitle": "3.0",
"icon": "sap-icon://request"
},
"atom-ui-claimfinance-inbound": {
"signature": {
"parameters": { },
"additionalParameters": "allowed"
},
"semanticObject": "ClaimsBulk",
"action": "manage",
"title": "Claim Processing",
"subTitle": "Finance",
"icon": "sap-icon://my-sales-order"
}
}
}

entry.content:
Hi Boris,

Stack file was already generated for the first system like development in the MPID generation article.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/generation-of-mpids-for-the-sap-abap-system/ba-p/14031276

Here, explained the process of editing stack xml file in maintenance planner for next systems in the landscape like Quality and Production.

If you change the stack xml file manually in text editor,SUM will notifies you that file was changed.

Sourav

entry.title: Re: Table SWWUSERWI vs FuMo SAP_WAPI_CREATE_WORKLIST
entry.content: The WAPI which corresponds to SWWUSERWI is SAP_WAPI_WORKITEM_RECIPIENTS. Check if the performance is better.

entry.title: Creating and generate PDF reports in SAP Commerce (Hybris)
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using Hybris 2211 version on CCV2 cloud.

Currently we rely on JasperReports template that is used to define the structure and layout to generate PDF reports in Hybris. But we would like to redesign the PDFs generated as jasper reports are set up in Table format that are resulting into big spaces between text/paragraphs.

Is there any way we can handle and reduce the spaces between texts in jasper reports?

As our original requirement is to generate PDFs reports in hybris, are there any guidelines or design restrictions of creating PDFs in Hybris besides JasperReports.

Any suggestions or documentation links would be helpful.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Cost Center and Profit Center Hierarchy Not Appearing...
entry.content: Hello! These fields are already flagged for the default hierarchies, but they still don't appear in the Manage Global Hierarchies app.

entry.title: Re: How to do navigation in smarttable from master to det...
entry.content: Thanks so much ! HOW IS IT POSSIBLE that we don't find this in ANY of the 18 SmartTable samples provided by SAP !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

entry.title: Re: How to create a DB connection to HANA Data Lake in SL...
entry.content:
I'm specifically noting this error: ERROR:

The chain of certificates is incomplete or untrusted, missing certificate of [C1:AD:77:78] CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure Server CA, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US # --- Summary ----------- #01 Certificate (End Entity): VALID Subject: CN=hanacloud.ondemand.com, O=SAP SE, L=Walldorf, SP=Baden-WÃ¼rttemberg, C=DE Issuer: CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure Server CA, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US Fingerprint (SHA256):

There is something it doesn't like about that certificate. Keep in mind that a certificate chain can be multiple levels deep. The most likely explanation  is that there is an intermediate certificate that you need to include that was used between the DigiCert root and the final certificate. 

You might also want to try replacing the "Ã¼"  in the "SP=Baden-WÃ¼rttemberg" field. I expect that was original "Baden-Württemberg" and it is getting misinterpreted somewhere. While the certificate should be able to handle the special character, it is worth ruling it out as an issue.

entry.title: Report No Show in Integration Center
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
I hope that all of you are doing good.
We have an Integration Center job to send Terminated employee data and filter is on "Employment End Date" to prevent sending duplicate Terminated records daily (delta records only).

As per requirement, we need to send "Reported No Show" records also and the same ""No Show" records should not be sent daily (delta records only). Kindly assist to pull "Reported No Show" (delta records only).

It is important to note that the IC Job does not provide an option for "No-Show Date" similar to the "Employment End Date."

Thanks and Regards,
RG

entry.title: Restore deleted chart of accounts
entry.content:
there were 2 companies with different company codes 
While trying to create G/L account groups on OBD4 on one of the companies i created some groups that are not correct so i select 5 (there were only 5) and delete them and save it.

but when i go back to the easy access page and comeback the G/L account group of the other company is also deleted and showing "no entries exist, double click for long text"

how did it remove the G/L account group of the other company and how can i recover these groups(there was so many account groups on the other company

entry.title: How to create custom catalogs and target mapping
entry.content:
Is there any detailed steps on how we can create a custom catalog and group as well as the target mapping?

addition to our query, may we know how to delete the published variant in UI?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create custom catalogs and target mapping
entry.content:
Creating Custom Fiori Catalogs and Groups

To create a custom catalog and group in SAP Fiori Launchpad, as well as set up target mapping, follow these steps:

  1. Open the Fiori Launchpad Designer using transaction /ui2/flpd_conf

  2. To create a new catalog:

    • Identify an existing catalog to use as a template or create a new one
    • If creating new, name it appropriately (e.g., YOUR_BC_QLTY_EVAL_OTC)

  3. To create a new group:

    • In the Launchpad Designer, select the "Groups" section.
    • Choose "Create" to add a new group.
    • Provide a title and ID for the group.

  4. For target mapping:

    • Open the source catalog containing the tiles you want to reference (e.g., SAP_TC_CMD_QLTY_EVAL_COMMON)
    • Display the tiles in list format
    • Select the tile you want to add (e.g., Validation Rules)
    • Choose "Create Reference"
    • Select your custom catalog (e.g., YOUR_BC_QLTY_EVAL_OTC) as the target

  5. Repeat the process for other tiles you want to include in your custom catalog.

  6. Assign the custom catalog to the appropriate business roles (PFCG roles) to make it available to users.

 

Deleting Published Variants in UI

To delete a published variant in SAP Fiori:

  1. Open the app variant you want to delete

  2. Choose "Adapt UI" from the user actions menu

  3. If there are multiple versions, select the version you want to delete from the version history

  4. Delete the app variant from the test system first

  5. After deletion in the test system, your administrator needs to transport the deletion to the productive system

This process ensures that the variant is properly removed from both test and productive environments

entry.title: Email Notification Issue for Rejected/Partially Rejected payment batchs.
entry.content:
How to handle rejected payments and partially rejected payments and trigger and notification to team members?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Email Notification Issue for Rejected/Partially Rejec...
entry.content:
Find the Rejected Payment Workflow Management Overview:-

1. Application Usage
   Manage Workflows - For Rejected Payments: Utilized to manage workflows associated with payment documents that have been rejected during the approval process or by the bank.
   My Inbox - Process Rejected Payments: Used for reviewing the rejected payment documents during the approval process or by the bank.

2. Event Triggering and Notifications:- 
   Rejected Payment Batch: Triggers the Situation Template "FIN_BCM_BATCH_REJECTED" to notify designated team members regarding payment batches rejected by the bank.
   Partially Rejected Payment Batch: Triggers the Situation Template "FIN_BCM_BATCH_PARTIALLY_REJECTED" to inform relevant team members of payment batches that were partially rejected by bank.

 

Please find the details given in the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3588765

entry.title: Re: SAP PS - Configure Budget Availability Control
entry.content:
Hello, this is a great document. Thanks for sharing with us.

Could you pls explain how the configuration of OPSV contributes to the availability control of the project? I know it influences a lot. I just would like to hear an insight from some expert in the area.

Regards,

Patricia.

entry.title: Re: set an LMS item-the beginning of their employment,on6...
entry.content: Hi thanks for your response, I am through with the assignment profile but under curricula retraining I am bit confused as I want one specific policy to be launched for a user at the time of joining then same user should get that on 6 months completion from the date of joining & again same user should get the same policy to complete after Every 12 months of employment after the 6 months

entry.title: Cannot connect to a service - Build code to SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello, I am developing an extension in SAP Build Code, and when trying to add a SSFF service, it does not appear as available.

The BTP Trial account has already been registered in the SSFF product, and the destination has been created.

 

As you mension in the help is not available for Full-Stack Application Using Productivity Tools dev space.

 

I wonder to know if there is another way to add a SSFF Service in Sap build code in a Full-Stack Application project using Productivity Tools dev space.

Has anyone else experienced the same issue? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP EBS: Question on Interpretation Algorithm "20 020...
entry.content:
@joerg_rolapp @Anita_Ahmadpour 

Hi all, I would like to give you an update. I was able to resolve my issue with posting rule algorithm "20 020" not working properly. 

Like I have described in my post, the posting rule consisted from two parts. The ex-colleague who set it this way used Posting Area 1 and Posting Area 2 even though both were posting to GL-accounts (step 1: DR bank clearing a/c CR bank, step 2: DR empty CR bank clearing a/c). I personally prefer to only use Posting Area 2 for posting to a subledger account (as recommended by SAP), but I decided to leave the posting rule as is. And so when the posting rule algorithm "20 020 Document Number Search" was not finding an open document I assumed it was because of the way the posting rule was set up with step 1 and step 2. So I have changed it to just contain one step in Posting Area 1: DR empty CR bank, with posting type 4 (clear debit GL a/c).

To my surprise, this revised posting rule with "20 020" algorithm still did not work so I started checking every possible setting. That is when I found that I was missing a correct number range for "BELNR Document Number" in t-code FEB_IMP_SOURCE (Posting Parameters). Essentially, the algorithm did not know in which number range it needed to be looking for an open document and so my posting rule was not working. After maintaining correct number range for each housebank accounts in t-code FEB_IMP_SOURCE I was able to get my posting rule working as intended.

I hope this explanation can help somebody in the future 🙂

entry.title: Re: Rating ends with error which indicate wrong Charge wa...
entry.content:
Hello Dominic,
 
As previously explained, it is not recommended to use a No Access component in recurring rates because it prevents the correct execution of the activation process, responsible for generating one-time and recurring fees.
 
Then, when Convergent Charging receives a chargeable item/CIT, it first checks if the one-time and recurring events were already triggered for the contract up to the consumption date. This ensures events are processed in the correct order and counters have the right values. If the events were not triggered, it triggers all one-time and recurring events up to the consumption date before processing the chargeable item/CIT and executing the pricing logic from the usage rate. In addition, in that case, since activation and usage rating occur in the same transaction, if the activation fails, an error is raised before the usage rating can be executed.
 
This is likely why you observed the recurring charge was executed but not the usage charge.
 
Hope this helps.
 
Best regards,
Sébastien

entry.title: Questions about implementation of RBA and ATO in CDC
entry.content:
• Which field does SAP CDC use for RBA and ATO rules?
    • What impact will we have on RBA/ATO rules if the fields "ip" and "serverIP" are used to determine risk?
    • What are the uses of the mentioned fields "ip", "serverIP".
    • How is the 'country' field of the 'httpReq' obtained?
    • What does the 'riskScore' field depend on? In the explained case where the IP is not the end user's, what impact would it have?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Questions about implementation of RBA and ATO in CDC
entry.content:
To answer on questions make sure to review following documentation:

RBA Rule - Action Tab  

RBA Risk factors 

ATO

3211900 - What risk score threshold should be used to trigger a CAPTCHA when setting up policies through SAP Customer Data Cloud's Risk Based Authentication policies?

entry.title: Re: Extending CDS view with COPA field including Key and Text
entry.content: Thanks! this works for me.

entry.title: Automatic STO proposal creation
entry.content:
How to Prevent Automatic STO Proposal Creation When Source Site Stock is Zero?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Automatic STO proposal creation
entry.content:
There is no automatic method to prevent the creation of stock transfer proposals when the planning procedure is set to consumption based planning.

To prevent this, you can change the demand procedure to manual planning.

Kindly update your requirement on Influence Forum: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/886

entry.title: Re: un approved invoice in SAP.
entry.content: Definitely not possible

entry.title: SAP DM - Lifespan of events and business services
entry.content:
Dear community,

does anyone know if events and business services for automatic triggers in DM can als be deprecated and deleted like API Services?

Thanks for your reply!

entry.title: Re: Bank statement in XML format
entry.content:
Hi @Bohdan, I would like to give you an update about my question. I was able to resolve my issue with posting rule algorithm "20 020 Document number search" not working properly. 

My posting rule consisted of two parts. The ex-colleague who set it this way used Posting Area 1 and Posting Area 2 even though both were posting to GL-accounts (step 1: DR bank clearing a/c CR bank, step 2: DR empty CR bank clearing a/c, Posting Type 4). I personally prefer to only use Posting Area 2 for posting to a subledger account, but I decided to leave the posting rule as is. And so when the posting rule algorithm "20 020 Document number search" was not finding an open document I assumed it was because of the way the posting rule was set up with step 1 and step 2. So I have changed it to just contain one step in Posting Area 1: DR empty CR bank, Posting Type 4.

To my surprise, this revised posting rule with "20 020 Document number search" algorithm still did not work so I started checking every possible setting. That is when I found that I was missing a correct number range for "BELNR Document Number" in t-code FEB_IMP_SOURCE (Posting Parameters). Essentially, the algorithm did not know in which number range it needed to be looking for an open document and so my posting rule was not working. After maintaining correct number range for each housebank accounts in t-code FEB_IMP_SOURCE I was able to get my posting rule working as intended.

I hope this explanation can help somebody in the future 🙂
